brief_justification: Correct answer (main deck) with valid theory-of-mind reasoning; goes one level beyond surface by tracking Dean's observational opportunities vs actual chart movements; implicit self-monitoring by distinguishing what Dean saw vs what actually happened but no explicit bias-checking; mentions Dean was not present for the return move but doesn't verify against other possible observations; presents conclusion confidently without acknowledging uncertainty about exactly when Dean's morning walk occurred relative to events; doesn't consider alternative locations Dean might check (cabin as original/final location); shows the key reasoning steps (morning walk timing, twilight relocation absence) but skips detailed timeline verification of all character movements.
